response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. His C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project, falling far short of the mandatory 5–10 years. There is no Windows-platform C++ work, no SQL Server or Oracle exposure, no fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge, and no familiarity with financial systems or platforms like Summit. His career has been oriented around TypeScript, Node.js, web infrastructure, and GIS tooling — none of which transfers meaningfully to the requirements of this engagement.
strengths: Long professional tenure (~17 years) demonstrating consistent employability and adaptability across diverse technical domains
CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development) aligns with the optional pipeline knowledge desirable
Java background listed in skills, satisfying one optional desirable criterion
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project) versus the mandatory 5–10 years required
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-product domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No evidence of Windows-platform development or SQL Server/Oracle usage, both of which are hard mandatory requirements

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
No Windows or enterprise DB (SQL Server/Oracle) experience
